title: How I Apply
summary: My resume, profile, and application strategy — owner workflow, not generic templates.

sections.title: Apply workflow
sections.summary: Build the resume and align LinkedIn and Naukri before I apply anywhere.
steps: Build resume (ChatGPT or any agent): Explain your full experience — companies, technologies, education, projects, employment history. State what AEM role you want (backend, frontend, campaign, etc.) and what kind of companies you are targeting. Share your skills. Ask for a tailored resume with ATS keywords that highlight your profile for those roles.

Do not use that export as-is. Go to Overleaf, pick a one-column resume template. Manually copy-paste content from the ChatGPT draft into LaTeX (easier than trusting ChatGPT-generated LaTeX — it often has errors). Fix any section that does not read right. Do not list GitHub, HackerRank, or other profile links unless you are actually active on them.
Upload your Overleaf PDF to ChatGPT and a few free ATS checker sites. If the score is above 70, that is good enough to proceed.

Upload the resume to ChatGPT and ask for steps to update LinkedIn and Naukri so they match the resume. Recruiters find you by keywords — skills and headline matter most. Share screenshots of each profile section and ask what to fill in (especially skills). Share your LinkedIn profile for a review. Spend real time on both profiles — they are mandatory. Use the same resume and profile details on any other job platforms. Update LWD, location, CTC, and other details accurately.
sections.body: I do not start applying until the resume passes ATS checks and LinkedIn and Naukri are aligned with it. Profile quality matters as much as the PDF.

sections.title: Outreach
sections.summary: Referrals, HR posts, and connection requests — not blind apply when I can reach a human.
steps: LinkedIn posts: Search AEM (or your role keyword), filter type to Posts. First pass — wider time window; after that — last 1 day only. Look for HR or hiring posts for your role. Often an email is in the post — send your application there. This works well because your profile goes directly to HR.

Gmail cover-letter template: For LinkedIn Invites or Naukri invites, keep a reusable Gmail template — short cover letter with skills, experience, LWD if applicable, contact details, and resume attached. Reuse for every invite-style apply.

Referral before direct apply: If I see a job on LinkedIn or Naukri, I do not apply immediately. I search that company on LinkedIn, find people in the same role (or close enough), and send a connection request. After they accept, I ask for a referral — not bluntly ("could you refer me?"). I use a ChatGPT-drafted template that mentions my role, experience, and why I fit. I wait for their reply. If referral works, great; if not, I can still apply later.
Recruiter direct: If the post lists a recruiter email or contact info, reaching out there often beats a generic portal apply.
sections.body: I treat outreach as higher signal than one-click apply. Templates live in Gmail; referral and HR-post wording I refine with ChatGPT once, then reuse.

sections.title: Learning sources
sections.summary: Technical prep vs job-search prep — I keep them separate.
steps: Job-search content (resume, LinkedIn, Naukri, referral messages): ChatGPT or any agent — with my real experience pasted in, not invented bullets.
AEM technical depth: This playbook's roadmaps, glossary, and interview prep — plus Experience League when I need to verify a topic before an interview.
Employer research: Target Companies table and company row evidence before I interview or message someone at that employer.
I do not claim tools or platforms on my resume or profiles unless I use them actively.
sections.body: ChatGPT optimizes how I present myself; this playbook optimizes what I can defend in a technical conversation.

sections.title: Weekly rhythm
sections.summary: How I search and apply on LinkedIn and Naukri after profiles are ready.
steps: First pass (one-time): On LinkedIn and Naukri, search your relevant role, set time filter to last 30 days, and apply only to postings that genuinely fit — batch this once when you start.
Ongoing daily search: After the 30-day backlog is cleared, search the same role with last 1 day only — apply daily to new listings.
LinkedIn posts: Search AEM (or your keyword), type Posts, wider window first then last 1 day on repeat visits. Check HR hiring posts and email applications (see Outreach).

Platform hygiene: Same search strategy on LinkedIn and Naukri. Update your availability on Naukri at least every 15 days. Keep refreshing or updating the Naukri profile — activity improves search visibility.
sections.body: Only after resume ATS pass and LinkedIn/Naukri are done. I combine portal apply with posts and referrals.

sections.title: Tools
sections.summary: What I actually use for resume, profiles, and applications.
steps: ChatGPT (or any LLM agent) — resume draft, ATS feedback, LinkedIn/Naukri optimization prompts, referral message templates.
Overleaf — one-column LaTeX resume template; manual paste from the draft, export PDF.
Free ATS checker sites — sanity check before I publish the resume (target 70+).
LinkedIn — profile, job search (30-day then 1-day filter), Posts search for HR hiring, company people search for referrals.
Naukri — profile mirror of LinkedIn, job search with same time-filter strategy, availability updates every ~15 days.
Gmail — saved template for cover letter + resume when applying via invite or email from a post.
This playbook — Target Companies, filters, and interview prep once conversations start.
sections.body: No apply bots. Every application uses the same polished resume and aligned profiles.
